name: Temporary Risk Acceptance Request - SLA Uptime.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
EXC-001  SLA Uptime Exception
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Temporary Risk Acceptance Request — SLA Uptime
Formal waiver for pilot-phase uptime risk pending commercial renegotiation.
Document ID
EXC-001
Owner
Vendor Management
Request Date
2024-03-15
Approval Status
Approved with conditions
Expiry
2024-06-30
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
Requested Exception
Meridian requests temporary acceptance of the current 99.5% monthly uptime commitment in the Northwind Cloud
Infrastructure SLA for the pilot window ending 2024-06-30. This exception is limited to availability targets and does not
waive unresolved liability or privacy issues.
Business Justification
●
Pilot launch timing depends on the Northwind managed retrieval platform becoming available before the Q2 executive
rollout.
●
Re-platforming to an alternative vendor within the current quarter would materially delay the internal AI search program.
●
The current service architecture includes compensating controls that reduce user-visible impact of isolated outages.
Compensating Controls
Control
Owner
Review Cadence
Dual-run fallback search path
Platform Engineering
Weekly
Monthly service report review and escalation
Vendor Management
Monthly
Executive checkpoint on revised SLA negotiation
Maria Santos
Bi-weekly
Approvals
Role
Name
Status / Date
VP, Procurement
Maria Santos
Approved — 2024-03-15
Chief Information Security Officer
Elena Petrov
Approved with conditions — 2024-03-15
Chief Operating Officer
Samuel Reed
Approved — 2024-03-16
